preview_text:
The model favors Czech Republic with a 54% win probability, placing them as clear but not overwhelming favorites. South Africa's 17% chance to win reflects their underdog status, while the draw probability of 29% sits between the two outcomes.

The spread suggests a competitive match with meaningful upset potential. Czech Republic's advantage is real but the 11-percentage-point gap to their loss probability leaves room for South Africa to emerge victorious or the match to finish level. A draw at 29% is plausible enough to warrant serious consideration alongside either team's outright win.

The model and sportsbook consensus are tightly aligned. Czech Republic win probabilities differ by less than 1 percentage point across all three sources. The draw sits slightly higher in the model at 29% versus 25–26% in the books and Polymarket, while South Africa's win probability is marginally higher in the sportsbooks. These gaps are within normal variance and suggest broad agreement on the match's competitive balance.
